top_findings.evidence: Screenshot shows hero h1 'SysNDD API' then Swagger block 'SysNDD API 0.21.6 OAS3.0'; ApiView.vue comment acknowledges Swagger's internal h2 cannot be suppressed.

top_findings.issue: Swagger's native typography/section headings ('default', 'health' in bright blue, large light 'SysNDD API') clash with the app's compact medical type scale; the embed feels visually foreign despite the font-family:inherit and link-color overrides.
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot: oversized thin 'SysNDD API' (~36px) and saturated-blue 'default'/'health' section labels differ from the 18-22px semibold app title scale; lighthouse color-contrast(5) on Swagger badges.

top_findings.issue: Remaining lighthouse color-contrast(5) + heading-order(1) originate inside the un-forkable Swagger bundle; the source adds #000-on-pastel method-badge and version-pill overrides, but the audit still counts Swagger-internal nodes.
top_findings.evidence: lighthouse a11y95 color-contrast(5); ApiView.vue lines 183-217 add :deep overrides but note Swagger internals cannot be fully controlled.

improvements.recommendation: Fix AppVersionInfo to unwrap Plumber array/quoted scalars (e.g. normalize version/commit/database via the existing unwrapValue util) so the card shows 'v0.21.6' and a clean 'unknown'/hidden commit instead of '[ "0.21.6" ]'.
improvements.expected_impact: Removes a high-visibility rendering bug shown on both /API and /About wherever AppVersionInfo is mounted.

improvements.recommendation: Hide or visually demote Swagger's internal info hgroup (the duplicate 'SysNDD API' title + version pill) via :deep, the same way .topbar is already display:none, so the hero h1 is the only product title.
improvements.expected_impact: Eliminates the duplicated title/version and tightens the top of the explorer.

improvements.recommendation: Constrain Swagger section-heading sizes and the 'default'/'health' tag label colors to the app type scale and medical-blue token via :deep so the embed reads as part of the app.
improvements.expected_impact: Brings the foreign Swagger typography closer to the compact clinical scale and reduces the saturated-blue clash.

improvements.recommendation: Document the residual Swagger-internal color-contrast(5)/heading-order(1) as a known third-party limitation in the page and the audit baseline, since they cannot be cleared without forking the bundle.
improvements.expected_impact: Sets correct expectations so the page is not re-penalized for un-fixable embed internals.

improvements.recommendation: Reuse the de-decorated AppVersionInfo surface (see About finding) so the hero version card is a plain bordered surface consistent with the shell rather than a bg-light card.
improvements.expected_impact: Visual consistency of the version widget across /API and /About.

one_line_verdict: Clean, trustworthy two-panel auth shell with labeled fields and tokenized buttons; the only real snag is an inverted visual heading weight (h2 'Sign in' reads larger than the route h1) that triggers the heading-order flag.

top_findings.issue: Visual/semantic heading mismatch: the left route-level <h1> 'Curator sign in' and right <h2> 'Sign in' both use the .login-title class, so the h2 renders as large/larger than the h1 — Lighthouse heading-order(1) and a confusing focal point (the form heading dominates the page heading).
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot shows 'Sign in' (right) at the same or greater visual size than 'Curator sign in' (left h1); LoginView.vue lines 21/45 both apply class="login-title" to h1 and h2.

top_findings.issue: Left context panel leans slightly marketing for a clinical tool: kicker label 'SYSNDD ACCOUNT', a feature bullet list with decorative bi-check/shield icons, and a descriptive paragraph before any action.
top_findings.evidence: LoginView.vue lines 20-38: login-kicker + login-description + login-feature-list; design intent asks to avoid decorative explanations before the data/action.

top_findings.issue: Field-level helper text ('Enter your user name' / 'Enter your user password') is redundant with the visible label and adds vertical noise without informational value.
top_findings.evidence: LoginView.vue lines 54 and 72 BFormGroup description props; visible under each input in the screenshot.

top_findings.issue: Large empty canvas: the two cards sit in the upper third leaving ~40% of the viewport empty below the fold, so the page reads sparse rather than focused.
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot: both panels end around y=610 of a 900px viewport with empty gradient below.

improvements.recommendation: Make the form panel heading a visually-subordinate <h2> (smaller/medium weight) or promote the left brand title to the single dominant h1 so size order matches DOM order — clears heading-order(1) and fixes the focal hierarchy.
improvements.expected_impact: Resolves the only a11y flag and makes the page-level identity (Curator sign in) the clear focal point.

improvements.recommendation: Trim the left context panel to one short trust line + the 'public stays free' note; drop the kicker and one bullet to align with the quiet clinical-tool intent.
improvements.expected_impact: Less marketing decoration, faster scan to the sign-in form.

improvements.recommendation: Remove the redundant per-field descriptions (or replace with only genuinely useful constraints, as Register does for char limits).
improvements.expected_impact: Tighter form rhythm, less visual noise.

improvements.recommendation: Vertically center the shell (or cap max-height and center) so the auth cards are not stranded in the top third on tall desktop viewports.
improvements.expected_impact: Both Login and Register feel intentional rather than sparse; shared .login-shell fix covers both.

one_line_verdict: Strong, fully-labeled application form brought to parity with Login — real labels, useful per-field constraints, ORCID/email validation, and a designed success overlay; clean a11y with no failing audits.

top_findings.issue: Same inverted heading weight as Login: left <h1> 'Apply for access' and right <h2> 'Register' both use .login-title, so the form heading competes with the page heading (no Lighthouse flag here, but the focal hierarchy is muddy).
top_findings.evidence: RegisterView.vue lines 60 and 90 both class="login-title"; screenshot shows 'Register' (h2) at the same visual scale as the h1.

top_findings.issue: The left context panel and right form are the same height-class but the form is much taller (7 fields + terms), so at desktop the left panel ends ~halfway and leaves a tall empty column beside the lower form fields.
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot: left panel content stops around 'Sign in here' (y~385) while the form extends past the fold; register-panel uses align-self:start (RegisterView.vue line 459).

top_findings.issue: Inputs are visually low-contrast/borderless-feeling (very light 1px borders on white) which, with placeholder-only affordance, makes the empty form read as a flat list of pale rectangles.
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot: each BFormInput is a faint outlined box; only the label distinguishes fields before focus.

top_findings.issue: Terms checkbox is initialized to 'not_accepted' and validated as required, which is correct, but the form gives no top-level summary of why submit is blocked when invalid — error feedback is per-field only.
top_findings.evidence: RegisterView.vue lines 206-226 (terms) and per-field BFormInvalidFeedback; no aggregated error summary region.

improvements.recommendation: Apply the same h2 de-emphasis used to fix Login so the page <h1> ('Apply for access') is unambiguously the largest element on both auth pages.
improvements.expected_impact: Consistent, correct heading hierarchy across the auth cluster from one shared rule.

improvements.recommendation: Add a sticky/short summary in the left panel (e.g. the 3 trust bullets pinned) or reduce form to a 2-column field grid for short fields (First/Family name) so the layout balances and the form is shorter.
improvements.expected_impact: Removes the tall empty left column and reduces perceived form length.

improvements.recommendation: Slightly strengthen input border contrast and add a focus ring token shared with the rest of the app so empty fields read as interactive controls, not static boxes.
improvements.expected_impact: Better field affordance across every form in the app.

improvements.recommendation: On failed submit, surface a single aria-live error summary above the actions listing the unmet fields, in addition to per-field feedback.
improvements.expected_impact: Clearer recovery path; helps screen-reader and keyboard users reach the blocking field.

one_line_verdict: Genuinely dense, clinical, scan-friendly information architecture (header → identifiers → entities → constraint/ClinVar/model-org triple → genomic viz) undercut hard by a massive aria-prohibited-attr violation and decorative gradient badges that fight the quiet-tool intent.

top_findings.issue: aria-prohibited-attr at 636 occurrences: GeneBadge/EntityBadge/DiseaseBadge render role="link" + aria-label on non-focusable <span>s (no href, no tabindex), and InheritanceBadge/CategoryIcon use role="img" on spans — repeated across the embedded entities table and identifier rows, this multiplies into hundreds of violations. role="link" on a span without a focusable/interactive context is the core defect.
top_findings.evidence: GeneBadge.vue lines 6-11 (role="link" :aria-label on span), EntityBadge.vue lines 6-14, DiseaseBadge.vue lines 6-12; Lighthouse aria-prohibited-attr(636!) on this page only.

top_findings.issue: Decorative skeuomorphic badges (linear-gradient fills, 1.5-2px colored borders, double box-shadows, italic text, hover translateY) contradict the SysNDD intent of flat, quiet pill chips and 'no heavy dark borders'; they read as a different design language than the tokenized cards around them.
top_findings.evidence: GeneBadge.vue lines 82-87 gradient+border+box-shadow; EntityBadge.vue lines 80-103; DiseaseBadge.vue lines 96-106 — versus the flat .border-subtle BCards and flat entity-chip pills in EntityView.

top_findings.issue: Protein/genomic visualization renders as a dense uniform band of ~hundreds of near-identical pink/red lollipop dots with no visible axis labels, position scale, legend, or pathogenicity color key in the captured state — the variant signal is illegible.
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot bottom 'Genomic Visualizations' / Protein View tab shows a flat red dot field with '2.6K variants' and no axis or legend visible.

top_findings.issue: CLS 0.198 still flagged: despite reserved min-heights on SectionCards, the header card (ClinicalResources + IdentifierCard) and the lazy genomic-viz tabs grow as async data and SVG render, shifting content.
top_findings.evidence: Lighthouse CLS 0.198; GeneView.vue header card has no reserved height while ClinicalResourcesCard/IdentifierCard hydrate (lines 50-62).

top_findings.issue: Heavy reliance on color-keyed ClinVar chips (P/LP/VUS/LB/B) is good, but the orange 'LP 133' chip uses dark text on #f97316 and the gnomAD amber CI bar (#ffc107) are borderline for the 1 remaining color-contrast flag.
top_findings.evidence: GeneClinVarCard.vue line 297 .clinvar-chip--lp background:#f97316 color:#111827; Lighthouse color-contrast(1).

improvements.recommendation: Fix the badge a11y at the component level: for non-link badges drop role="link"/aria-label and let the visible text be the name; when linkTo is set, render a real <a>/<RouterLink> (focusable) so naming is valid. Convert role="img" decorative badges to plain spans with the text/title carrying the meaning. This one shared fix clears the 636-count violation everywhere badges appear (tables, gene, entity).
improvements.expected_impact: Eliminates the single largest a11y defect in the app and lifts gene-detail accessibility from ~4 toward parity with the 100-score pages.

improvements.recommendation: Re-skin GeneBadge/EntityBadge/DiseaseBadge/InheritanceBadge to flat tokenized pills (solid token color or tinted background, 1px subtle border, no gradient/inset shadow/italic) matching the EntityView .entity-chip language.
improvements.expected_impact: Unifies the chip vocabulary across every page and removes the marketing-skeuomorphic look that violates the design intent.

improvements.recommendation: Add axis ticks (amino-acid position), a domain track, and a pathogenicity color legend to the protein lollipop view, plus density binning/jitter so overlapping variants are readable; show a designed empty/too-dense state.
improvements.expected_impact: Makes the headline genomic visualization interpretable instead of a red smear.

improvements.recommendation: Reserve explicit min-heights for the gene header card (resources + identifier rows) and the genomic-viz tab panel/SVG region so async hydration does not push the page; verify against the CLS budget.
improvements.expected_impact: Drives CLS under 0.1 and stops the visible jump when ClinVar/SVG data arrives.

improvements.recommendation: Darken or restyle the LP (#f97316) chip and amber CI bar to meet 4.5:1 (e.g. deepen to a #d9480f-class orange with white text), tokenizing the ACMG palette.
improvements.expected_impact: Clears the last color-contrast flag and makes the ACMG scale reusable everywhere ClinVar appears.

one_line_verdict: A reference-quality compact clinical record: the Gene·Inheritance·Disease triple-cell unit, metadata pill row, and well-designed empty states make the entity legible at a glance; only the gradient badges and a heading-order nit hold it back.

top_findings.issue: The gene/disease/entity gradient badges (GeneBadge green, DiseaseBadge gray, EntityBadge blue) carry the same role="link"+aria-label-on-span pattern and skeuomorphic gradient styling as gene-detail — they sit beside the otherwise flat, tokenized entity-chip pills, creating two chip languages on one page.
top_findings.evidence: EntityView.vue lines 29-62 use EntityBadge/GeneBadge/DiseaseBadge/InheritanceBadge; their gradient/role styling (GeneBadge.vue 82-87) differs from the flat .entity-chip pills (EntityView.vue 649-721).

top_findings.issue: Heading-order(1): the route <h1> is 'Entity sysndd:2' inside the SectionCard header, while the four evidence cards and Clinical Synopsis use plain bold <span> labels (not headings), so AT users get one h1 and then non-heading section titles with no h2 scaffolding.
top_findings.evidence: EntityView.vue line 27 h1; section titles rendered as SectionCard .section-card-title spans (SectionCard.vue lines 96-100) and clinical-card-header span (line 105).

top_findings.issue: This entity is data-sparse (synopsis 'NA', no publications, no phenotypes, only one GeneReview + one variation term), so most cards show empty states — well handled, but the four-card evidence row leaves three large near-empty white panels that read as wasted space.
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot: Publications 'No publications linked.', Phenotypes 'No phenotype terms linked.', tall empty panels at min-height 18rem (EntityView.vue line 205).

top_findings.issue: 5 failed network requests in the page load (per Lighthouse note) — likely the gene-link / status / review enrichment hooks 404-ing for this sparse entity; no user-visible breakage but it adds console noise and risks flicker.
top_findings.evidence: Lighthouse '+5 failed network requests'; EntityView fires six entity hooks + useGeneRecord on tick 0 (EntityView.vue lines 302-317).

top_findings.issue: The 'Classification: not applicable' pill and 'NDD No' pill pair icon+label correctly (good, not color-alone), but the synopsis card reserves 18rem and shows only 'NA' — a lot of vertical emptiness for a single token of content.
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot: Clinical Synopsis card is the tallest element yet contains only 'NA'; EntityView.vue min-height 18rem line 101.

improvements.recommendation: Adopt the shared flat-pill badge restyle (same foundation fix as gene-detail) so GeneBadge/EntityBadge/DiseaseBadge match the .entity-chip vocabulary already used on this page — one chip language per page.
improvements.expected_impact: Visual consistency on the entity page and everywhere badges co-exist with chips.

improvements.recommendation: Render SectionCard titles as a configurable heading level (e.g. h2) so each card title participates in the heading outline under the single h1, clearing heading-order across all detail pages.
improvements.expected_impact: Fixes heading-order on entity-detail and any other SectionCard-based page in one change.

improvements.recommendation: Collapse or auto-shrink empty evidence cards (reduce reserved min-height when empty, or merge sparse cards into a single 'Linked evidence' panel) so data-poor entities don't show three tall blank boxes.
improvements.expected_impact: Sparse entities look intentional and compact instead of underpopulated.

improvements.recommendation: Audit the entity hooks so optional sub-resources that legitimately have no data resolve as empty (200/empty) rather than throwing 404 network failures, and ensure failures stay silent in the SectionCard empty state.
improvements.expected_impact: Removes the 5 failed requests, cuts console noise, and avoids any error-state flicker.

improvements.recommendation: Shrink the Clinical Synopsis reserved height when the synopsis is short/NA (let it grow only when content exists), keeping the card honest about its content volume.
improvements.expected_impact: Removes the large empty synopsis block on minimal records while preserving CLS reservation when real synopsis text is present.
